notes: Here, accused of traitorous conspiracy against his sovereign by king Saul, or by Cush one of his courtiers.

quotes.text: Seven-part symmetric schemes, so common elsewhere in the Hebrew Bible, are relatively scarce in the Psalter. There are, however, a few clear examples of this configuration. Like three-part and five-part chiasmuses, seven-part symmetries allow for emphasis through matched repetition and through the highlighted central position. The symmetric structure of Psalm 7 underscores the contrast between the psalmist's innocence and his enemies' guilt. and highlights Yahweh's role as righteous judge. The psalm's center is the thematic center of the psalm: the psalmist's appeal to Yahweh to vindicate him and condemn his wicked enemies
quotes.speaker: David A. Dorsey
quotes.source: The Literary Structure of the Old Testament
quotes.attr: David A. Dorsey, The Literary Structure of the Old Testament
